section: RADIATION TESTS OF A MINIATURE, RADIATION-RESISTANT TV CAMERA
text: In the first test, the camera was equipped with a standard lens and lowered into a $1.5 \times 10^{6} \mathrm{r/hr}$ gamma field.

section: RADIATION TESTS OF A MINIATURE, RADIATION-RESISTANT TV CAMERA
text: There was no observable initial effect as the camera was lowered into the field; however, the picture faded rapidly and required maximum target voltage after 20 min of operation.

section: RADIATION TESTS OF A MINIATURE, RADIATION-RESISTANT TV CAMERA
text: After 1 hr of additional exposure in a reduced field of approximately $5 \times 10^{5} \mathrm{r}/\text{hour}$ , the picture was not readable.

section: RADIATION TESTS OF A MINIATURE, RADIATION-RESISTANT TV CAMERA
text: An additional 18 hr of exposure was accumulated at this lower level before the camera was removed from the radiation field.

section: RADIATION TESTS OF A MINIATURE, RADIATION-RESISTANT TV CAMERA
text: The total dose was $5 \times 10^{6} \mathrm{r}$ .

section: RADIATION TESTS OF A MINIATURE, RADIATION-RESISTANT TV CAMERA
text: In the second test, the camera was equipped with a nonbrowning lens and lowered into a $3.9 \times 10^{5} \mathrm{r/hr}$ gamma field.

section: RADIATION TESTS OF A MINIATURE, RADIATION-RESISTANT TV CAMERA
text: The duration of the test was approximately 250 hr, and the total integrated dose received by the camera was approximately $10^{8} \mathrm{r}$ .

section: RADIATION TESTS OF A MINIATURE, RADIATION-RESISTANT TV CAMERA
text: Subsequent tests showed that approximately $20\%$ of the loss of sensitivity was due to browning of the camera lens and the lamp bulbs.

text: The remaining $80\%$ was apparently due to browning of the vidicon faceplate.

section: RADIATION TESTS OF A MINIATURE, RADIATION-RESISTANT TV CAMERA
text: Because of the time and expense involved, and because the $10^{8} \mathrm{r}$ dosage obtained exceeded the dose expected in the HRT viewing operation, no attempt was made to determine the ultimate life of the camera.

section: MOLTEN-SALT REACTOR EXPERIMENT
text: Figure 6 shows a Kintel, model 25ll radiation-resistant camera presently used in maintenance operations at the Molten-Salt Reactor Experiment (MSRE).

section: MOLTEN-SALT REACTOR EXPERIMENT
text: The cameras are radiation and shock resistant, and will withstand continuous exposure to 1-Mev gamma radiation at a dose rate of about $10^{5}$ r/hr for 100 hr without degradation of performance.

section: MOLTEN-SALT REACTOR EXPERIMENT
text: Replacement of the vidicon tube and the lens will restore the camera performance until the camera has accumulated a radiation dosage of $10^{5}$ r.

section: MOLTEN-SALT REACTOR EXPERIMENT
text: Each camera is connected by 50 ft of radiation-resistant cable to a pre-amplifier which, in turn, is connected by a 100-ft cable to a camera control unit.
